chapter.label: Tale 130
chapter.title: Admonitions Of Abba Athanasios And His Wondrous Vision

paragraphs.text: This same Abba Athanasios said: ‘Our fathers maintained selfdiscipline and indifference to worldly goods,* but we have lined well both our bellies and our purses’.
Again: ‘In our fathers’ time it was very important to avoid distractions. Now our cooking pot and our handwork rule us’.

He also told us this: One day, this thought came into my mind: what difTerence does it make whether we fight the fight agdnizein or not? I fell as though into a trance and ong came who said to me: ‘Follow me’. He i led me into a place filled with light and stood me before a door, the { beauty of which was beyond description. We were hearing what sounded like an innumerable multitude within, praising God. We knocked and somebody inside heard us and cried: ‘What do you want? My guide replied: ‘We want to come in'. The other answered, saying to me: Nobody comes in here who lives negligently en ameleiai. I you want to come in, go and fight the (ight, holding nothing to be of any acconnt in the vain world’.
